-------------------

1. INTRODUCTION

1.1 Overview

Hats Protocol is a decentralized protocol for creating and managing on-chain organizational roles and responsibilities. It enables organizations to create, assign, and revoke authority in a flexible and composable way.

Key Features:
- Dynamic role-based access control
- Programmable authority delegation
- Composable organizational structures
- Multi-chain deployment
- Extensible module system

1.2 Key Concepts

Hats:
A Hat represents a role or position within an organization. Each Hat can have specific powers, responsibilities, and eligibility criteria. Hats are non-transferable and can be revoked based on predefined conditions.

Properties:
- Unique identifier (Hat ID)
- Eligibility criteria
- Toggle conditions
- Administrative relationships
- Associated powers and responsibilities

Wearing a Hat:
When an address "wears" a Hat, it means they have been granted the authority and responsibilities associated with that role. Hat-wearing is dynamic and can be automatically revoked based on eligibility conditions.

Tree Structure:
Hats are organized in a tree structure where:
- Each Hat can have multiple child Hats
- Child Hats can only be administered by addresses wearing their parent Hat
- The tree structure enables natural organizational hierarchies

1.3 Architecture

The Hats Protocol architecture consists of several key components:

Core Protocol:
- Smart contracts managing Hat creation and wearing
- Eligibility and revocation logic
- Administrative functions
- Events and hooks system

Modules:
- Extensible system for custom functionality
- Pre-built modules for common use cases
- Module registry and verification

Integration Points:
- SDK libraries for different languages
- Subgraph data access
- Web3 interfaces
- Cross-chain compatibility

-------------------

2. PROTOCOL SPECIFICATION

2.1 Core Protocol

The core protocol is implemented as a set of smart contracts that manage the creation, assignment, and revocation of Hats. The protocol is designed to be:

- Minimal: Core functionality is kept simple and focused
- Extensible: Additional features can be added through modules
- Secure: Critical operations are protected by robust access controls
- Gas-efficient: Operations are optimized for minimal gas usage

Core Functions:
- createHat: Create new Hats in the tree
- mintHat: Assign a Hat to an address
- transferHat: Transfer Hat wearing status (if allowed)
- checkEligibility: Verify if an address can wear a Hat
- toggleHat: Enable/disable Hat wearing
- burnHat: Remove a Hat from an address

2.2 Smart Contracts

The core smart contracts of Hats Protocol include:

HatsProtocol.sol:
- Main contract implementing core Hat functionality
- Manages Hat creation, minting, and burning
- Handles eligibility and toggle logic
- Emits events for state changes

HatsModules:
- Base contracts for extending functionality
- Standardized interfaces for module integration
- Security patterns and best practices

2.3 Events and Hooks

The protocol emits events for all significant state changes:
- HatCreated
- HatMinted
- HatBurned
- HatToggled
- EligibilityChanged
- ModuleAdded
- ModuleRemoved

Hook System:
- Pre and post operation hooks
- Custom logic injection points
- Module integration interfaces

-------------------

4. SUBGRAPHS AND DATA ACCESS

4.1 Available Endpoints

Hosted Endpoints (Free):
- Ethereum (mainnet): https://api.studio.thegraph.com/query/55784/hats-v1-ethereum/version/latest
- Arbitrum: https://api.studio.thegraph.com/query/55784/hats-v1-arbitrum/version/latest
- Base: https://api.studio.thegraph.com/query/55784/hats-v1-base/version/latest
- Celo: https://api.studio.thegraph.com/query/55784/hats-v1-celo/version/latest
- Gnosis: https://api.studio.thegraph.com/query/55784/hats-v1-gnosis-chain/version/latest
- Optimism: https://api.studio.thegraph.com/query/55784/hats-v1-optimism/version/latest
- Polygon: https://api.studio.thegraph.com/query/55784/hats-v1-polygon/version/latest
- Sepolia: https://api.studio.thegraph.com/query/55784/hats-v1-sepolia/version/latest

The Graph Network Endpoints:
- Ethereum (mainnet): https://thegraph.com/explorer/subgraphs/AtrhAMCcVfPbmejxTez3G59Kdfu5tMFoiPsTUjdCzpKx
- Arbitrum: https://thegraph.com/explorer/subgraphs/4CiXQPjzKshBbyK2dgJiknTNWcj8cGUJsopTsXfm5HEk
- Other networks available (see documentation)

-------------------

6. SECURITY AND AUDITS

6.1 Security Model

The Hats Protocol security model is built on several key principles:

1. Least Privilege
- Roles have minimal necessary permissions
- Permissions are explicitly granted
- Dynamic revocation of authority

2. Hierarchical Control
- Parent Hats control child Hats
- Clear authority chains
- Immutable administrative relationships

3. Composable Security
- Modular security components
- Flexible integration patterns
- Extensible through modules

6.2 Audit Reports

The protocol has undergone multiple security audits:

Trust Security Audit:
- Date: February 23, 2023
- Commit: 60f07df
- Scope: Core protocol and modules
- Status: All findings addressed

Sherlock Audit:
- Date: May 3, 2023
- Commit: fafcfd
- Scope: Core protocol
- Status: All findings addressed

-------------------

7. ADVANCED TOPICS

7.1 Hats Signer Gate

Hats Signer Gate (HSG) v2 is a Zodiac module that enables:
- Dynamic multisig management based on Hat wearing
- Automatic signer addition/removal
- Threshold management within specified ranges
- Transaction guard functionality

Features:
1. Signer Management
- Grants signing rights based on Hat wearing
- Removes invalid signers automatically
- Manages multisig threshold dynamically

2. Security Guards
- Prevents invalid signer execution
- Protects critical Safe configuration
- Restricts delegatecall targets

3. Owner Controls
- Threshold range configuration
- Target contract approval
- Module management

[continued in subsequent sections...] 

3. SDKs AND INTEGRATION

3.1 V1 SDK

The V1 SDK provides comprehensive tooling for interacting with Hats Protocol:

Core SDK:
- Contract interaction utilities
- Hat management functions
- Event handling
- Type definitions

Features:
- TypeScript/JavaScript support
- Full type safety
- Chain-agnostic design
- Comprehensive documentation

Installation:
```bash
npm install @hatsprotocol/sdk-v1-core
```

3.2 Modules SDK

The Modules SDK enables development and integration of Hats Protocol modules:

Features:
- Module creation utilities
- Standard interfaces
- Testing utilities
- Example implementations

Components:
- Base module contracts
- Integration patterns
- Security templates
- Event handlers

3.3 Account SDK

The Account SDK provides tools for managing Hats-based accounts:

Features:
- Account creation and management
- Signer management
- Transaction handling
- Multi-chain support

Integration Points:
- Safe compatibility
- Module integration
- Custom implementations
- Cross-chain operations

3.4 Integration Guides

Best Practices:
1. Environment Setup
- Network configuration
- Provider setup
- Type generation

2. Contract Interaction
- Reading Hat state
- Creating and managing Hats
- Handling events
- Error handling

3. Module Integration
- Module deployment
- Configuration
- Testing and validation
- Security considerations

4. Account Management
- Account creation
- Signer management
- Transaction handling
- Security best practices

-------------------

8. REFERENCE

8.1 API Reference

Core Protocol:
```solidity
function createHat(
    uint256 admin,
    string calldata details,
    uint32 maxSupply,
    address eligibility,
    address toggle,
    bool mutable_,
    string calldata imageURI
) external returns (uint256 hatId)
```

```solidity
function mintHat(
    uint256 hatId,
    address wearer
) external
```

[Additional function references...]

8.2 Error Codes

Common Errors:
- INVALID_HAT
- NOT_ADMIN
- NOT_WEARER
- EXCEEDS_MAX_SUPPLY
- IMMUTABLE_HAT
- INVALID_ELIGIBILITY
- INVALID_TOGGLE

8.3 Glossary

Terms:
- Hat: A role or position within an organization
- Wearer: An address that has been granted a Hat
- Admin: A Hat that can manage child Hats
- Eligibility: Conditions for wearing a Hat
- Toggle: Conditions for enabling/disabling a Hat
- Module: Extension of core functionality
- Tree: Hierarchical organization of Hats
